title: Replace photo frame overlay with premium border and add WhatsApp sharing button

requirements.summary: Remove overlapping SVG filigree frame from bride/groom photos and replace with premium CSS border styling
acceptanceCriteria: No SVG filigree or overlay element renders on top of or across the bride/groom photo area.
The photo is fully visible without any element covering any portion of the image.
A premium-looking border/boundary is visible around the outside edge of each photo container.
The border uses gold/saffron tones consistent with the app's Hindu wedding theme.
The border styling looks premium across all 8 templates in both light and dark variants.
The floating 3D bobbing animation on the photo frame container is still present and functional.
The reduce-motion toggle still disables the bobbing animation when enabled.

file_operations.description: Remove the filigree frame overlay image (frontend/public/assets/generated/filigree-frame.dim_600x800.png) that renders on top of the bride/groom photos. Replace the overlaid frame with a premium multi-layered CSS border applied directly to the photo container using a combination of border, box-shadow, and outline properties. Style the border with gold/saffron tones (using theme colors from the app's palette), rounded corners via border-radius, and subtle inset/outer glow effects. Preserve the existing floating 3D bobbing animation logic and reduced-motion detection. Ensure the photo image is always fully visible without any overlapping decorative elements.

Update the couple photos section where FloatingPhotoFrame is used to ensure the new premium CSS border styling is consistent across all template variants. Verify that the border styling works correctly in both light and dark theme modes. If there are any hardcoded frame-related styles or wrapper elements specific to the old overlay approach, remove or adjust them to support the new border-only design. Ensure the floating animation remains functional and that the reduce-motion preference is respected.

requirements.summary: Add WhatsApp sharing button to invitation page with couple names, wedding date, and invitation URL
acceptanceCriteria: A WhatsApp sharing button is visible on every published invitation page at /invite/:id.
Clicking the button opens a new browser tab with a wa.me share URL.
The WhatsApp share message includes the couple names, wedding date, and the full invitation URL.
The button is styled consistently with the app's premium Hindu wedding theme (gold accents, matching typography).
The button is responsive and visible on both mobile and desktop viewports.
The WhatsApp icon or label clearly identifies the button as a WhatsApp share action.

file_operations.description: Add a WhatsApp sharing button alongside the existing QR code and copyable URL. When clicked, the button should construct a WhatsApp share URL in the format https://wa.me/?text=<encoded message> where the message includes the couple names (from invite.coupleNames), the formatted wedding date (using the existing date formatting utility from lib/utils.ts), and the full invitation URL (window.location.href or constructed from the invite ID). Open the WhatsApp URL in a new browser tab using window.open or target="_blank". Style the button to match the app's premium theme using gold/saffron accents, Cormorant Garamond or Cinzel typography, and ensure it is responsive. Use a recognizable WhatsApp icon (either an SVG or a Unicode symbol) to clearly identify the button's purpose. Place the button near the existing share controls.
